title: Coefficients
summary: Defines abstract composition coefficients and a placeholder closed coefficient system.

mathematicalContent.heading: Mathematical role
mathematicalContent.body: Defines abstract composition coefficients and a placeholder closed coefficient system.

mathematicalContent.heading: How to read the translation
mathematicalContent.body: The correspondence below follows Juhlformula/Juhloperators/Coefficients.lean declaration by declaration. A definition is translated by its defining expression, a theorem by its quantified proposition, and a structure, class, or inductive type by the mathematical data encoded in its fields or constructors. The original Lean signature remains visible beside every translation.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Abstract coefficient system for Juhl-type formulas. mCoeff and nCoeff are kept separate because Juhl formulas often use one family for the direct formula and another for the inverse formula. Its mathematical data are: m Coeff: for every N : the natural numbers, Composition N maps to ℚ; n Coeff: for every N : the natural numbers, Composition N maps to ℚ.
Singleton coefficient condition. In explicit terms, for c of type coefficient system, its defining expression is for every N h N : 0 < N, C.n Coeff Composition.singleton N h N equals 1.

Pair coefficient accessor. In explicit terms, for c of type coefficient system, a, b in the natural numbers, ha of type 0 < a, and hb of type 0 < b, its defining expression is C.n Coeff Composition.pair a b ha hb.
correspondence.leanType: def pairNCoeff (C : CoefficientSystem) (a b : ℕ) (ha : 0 < a) (hb : 0 < b) : ℚ

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Initial partial sums of the parts of a composition. In explicit terms, for n in the natural numbers, and i of type composition n, its defining expression is I.parts.scanl · plus · 0.
correspondence.leanType: def partialSums {N : ℕ} (I : Composition N) : List ℕ

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Product of parts as a rational number. In explicit terms, for n in the natural numbers, and i of type composition n, its defining expression is I.parts.foldl fun acc : ℚ n maps to acc times n : ℚ 1 : ℚ.
correspondence.leanType: def partsProdQ {N : ℕ} (I : Composition N) : ℚ

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Factorial product of parts as a rational number. In explicit terms, for n in the natural numbers, and i of type composition n, its defining expression is I.parts.foldl fun acc : ℚ n maps to acc times Nat.factorial n : ℚ 1 : ℚ.
correspondence.leanType: def factorialPartsProdQ {N : ℕ} (I : Composition N) : ℚ

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Closed-form Juhl m coefficient placeholder. This intentionally keeps the interface stable while the exact product normalization is fixed against the chosen Juhl convention. In explicit terms, for n in the natural numbers, and i of type composition n, its defining expression is 1.
correspondence.leanType: def mCoeffQ {N : ℕ} (_I : Composition N) : ℚ

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Closed-form Juhl n coefficient placeholder. This intentionally keeps the interface stable while the exact inverse coefficient formula is introduced later. In explicit terms, for n in the natural numbers, and i of type composition n, its defining expression is 1.
correspondence.leanType: def nCoeffQ {N : ℕ} (_I : Composition N) : ℚ

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The closed-form coefficient system. In explicit terms, its defining expression is m Coeff : equals the function sending I to m Coeff Q I n Coeff : equals the function sending I to n Coeff Q I.
For N in the natural numbers, and hN of type 0 < N, n Coeff Q Composition.singleton N h N equals 1.
correspondence.leanType: theorem nCoeffQ_singleton (N : ℕ) (hN : 0 < N) : nCoeffQ (Composition.singleton N hN) = 1

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Coefficient System.Singleton Normalized closed Coefficient System.

references.note: Source for ordered GJMS compositions and coefficients.

references.locator: Direct ambient proof of Juhl's formulas
references.note: Ambient interpretation of the composition formula.

limitations: Verified means that Lean checks the propositions encoded in this file and their imported assumptions. It does not assert that the present formal statement exhausts every analytic hypothesis or convention in the cited literature.
